description: Tax taxonomy and keyword dictionary for the WHT Beneficial Owner Agent. Authoritative controlled vocabulary for Polish withholding tax analysis. Used for: RAG query expansion, document chunking by concept, substance coverage scoring, interview question generation, entity pre-classification, and cross-module reuse in Tax OS.
review_status: initial_draft — legal accuracy to be verified by tax professional

usage.rag_query_expansion: Before vector retrieval, expand the agent query by appending rag_keywords from all matched concept IDs. This converts 'is this entity a beneficial owner?' into a query that includes 'rzeczywisty właściciel', 'Art. 4a pkt 29', 'warunek i ii iii', etc.
usage.document_chunking: When indexing legal texts (MF Objaśnienia, CIT Act), split at section boundaries corresponding to concept IDs. Tag each chunk with its concept IDs so retrieval is concept-level rather than semantic-similarity-level.
usage.coverage_scoring: After agent produces a substance conclusion, compare the concept IDs it referenced against the required_concept_ids for the entity_type. Missing concept IDs are gaps — surface them in the report.
usage.interview_question_generation: Each substance_factors concept maps to one or more SubstanceInterviewer questions. When new criteria are added to the taxonomy, the interviewer can auto-generate questions from them.
usage.entity_preclassification: Match entity name patterns against entity_classification concept name_indicators before analysis starts. Provides prior risk context to the agent.
usage.cross_module_reuse: Concepts shared between WHT (Phase 9) and future Tax OS modules (Pillar Two, PE, TP) are tagged with module_relevance. One definition, multiple modules.

categories.bo_test: The three-condition beneficial owner test under Art. 4a pkt 29 Polish CIT Act
categories.entity_classification: Types of legal entities relevant to WHT and substance analysis
categories.payment_types: Types of cross-border income subject to Polish WHT
categories.substance_factors: Substance criteria per MF Objaśnienia 2025 §2.3 — applied in check_entity_substance
categories.conduit_indicators: Red-flag indicators of conduit entity status per MF Objaśnienia §2.2.1
categories.mli_ppt: MLI Article 7 Principal Purpose Test and related anti-avoidance concepts
categories.treaty_concepts: Double taxation convention mechanics, rates, and residence concepts
categories.dempe: OECD BEPS Actions 8–10 DEMPE framework for intangible asset analysis
categories.compliance_mechanisms: Polish WHT compliance pathways: statutory rate, treaty rate, EU Directives, Pay and Refund
categories.due_diligence: Documentation and due diligence obligations on the Polish paying entity
categories.risk_classification: WHT risk tiers used for third-party vendor and intercompany screening
categories.confidence_levels: Data quality and confidence assessment for agent conclusions
categories.pillar_two_sttr: OECD Pillar Two Subject to Tax Rule (STTR) and Global Minimum Tax impacts
categories.digital_tax_compliance: 2026 Digital compliance mandates: KSeF (e-Invoicing), Payroll/Lease registries
categories.board_governance: Governance and decision-making independence metrics (Board Saturation)

concepts.definition: Mandatory structured e-invoicing in Poland as of Feb 1, 2026. Payments must link to a verified KSeF Invoice ID. Lack of KSeF match is a high-signal non-compliance flag in 2026 audits.

OECD Pillar Two rule allowing Poland to levy a 'Top-Up' WHT if the recipient's effective tax rate (ETR) in its home jurisdiction is below 9%. Overrides standard DTT benefits for certain intragroup payments (interest, royalties).
Risk indicator where a local director sits on >10 boards. KAS flags such entities as lacking autonomous decision-making (Condition III/BO). High signal for shell/conduit structures in 2026.
2026 audit approach focusing on technical evidence: board meeting IP origins (via MS Teams/Zoom logs) vs. residency claims, IT infrastructure costs (P&L server/dev costs), and KSeF links.

2026 risk where KAS recharacterizes 'Management Fees' as 'Dividends' or 'Royalties' to trigger 19%/20% WHT. Requires TP benchmarking in a Local File to defend 'Business Profit' (Art. 7) classification.

An entity that receives a payment for its own benefit, is not obliged to pass it on, and carries on genuine business activity in its country of residence. Defined by three cumulative conditions under Art. 4a pkt 29 Polish CIT Act.
concepts.review_note: Verify Polish statutory definition matches Art. 4a pkt 29 Dz.U. 2026 poz. 331

concepts.term_en: condition I — economic and legal title to income
concepts.term_pl: warunek I — tytuł prawny i ekonomiczny do należności
concepts.definition: The entity receives the payment as the rightful legal and economic owner of the underlying asset or right — not merely as an agent, nominee, or intermediary. Condition (i) of Art. 4a pkt 29 CIT.
concepts.review_note: Confirm exact wording in Art. 4a pkt 29 lit. a — is it 'lit. a' or numbered differently?

concepts.term_pl: warunek II — brak obowiązku dalszego przekazania
concepts.definition: The entity is not contractually or factually obliged to forward the received income to a third party. Either a formal contractual pass-through obligation or a factual/informal arrangement that achieves the same economic result disqualifies BO status. Condition (ii) of Art. 4a pkt 29 CIT. Key battleground in Polish WHT disputes.
concepts.review_note: MF Objaśnienia §2.2.1 expands on what constitutes 'factual' pass-through — verify exact criteria

concepts.term_en: condition III — genuine business activity in country of residence
concepts.term_pl: warunek III — prowadzenie rzeczywistej działalności gospodarczej w kraju siedziby
concepts.definition: The entity carries on genuine business activity in its country of residence — not merely a formal registered address or letterbox. This is the substance condition and the most fact-intensive of the three. Assessed against MF Objaśnienia §2.3 universal criteria. Condition (iii) of Art. 4a pkt 29 CIT.
concepts.review_note: MF Objaśnienia §2.3 lists universal criteria + §2.3.1 holding-company-specific criteria — verify both

concepts.definition: A company whose principal activity is to hold shares in subsidiaries or other assets. For WHT purposes, a holding company faces higher substance scrutiny because its 'business activity' is asset management rather than trade. MF Objaśnienia §2.3.1 specifies holding-company-specific substance criteria in addition to the universal ones.
key_provisions: MF Objaśnienia §2.3.1 — holding company specific substance criteria
concepts.review_note: Confirm §2.3.1 specific criteria list in MF Objaśnienia — what exact factors for holding companies?

concepts.definition: A company that carries on active commercial, industrial, or service business. For WHT purposes, operating companies are presumed to have stronger substance than pure holding companies. Substance assessment still required but default assumption is more favourable.

A company whose principal asset is intellectual property (patents, trademarks, know-how, software) that it licences to group or third-party entities. For WHT purposes, royalty payments to IP holdings face both the BO substance test AND the DEMPE analysis to verify functional alignment with income.

A company that on-lends funds within a corporate group, acting as the group's internal bank. For WHT purposes, interest payments to finance companies face BO scrutiny because the economic substance of lending may actually sit at the parent/shareholder level, not in the finance vehicle.
key_provisions: Art. 21 ust. 1 CIT — WHT on interest

concepts.definition: An entity interposed in a payment chain primarily to access a more favourable tax treaty or directive, without genuine substance in its country of residence. Fails the BO test, typically on condition (ii) or (iii) or both. The central concept the WHT agent is designed to detect.

An entity that is related to the Polish paying entity through common ownership, control, or management, as defined for transfer pricing purposes. Triggers the highest due diligence standard under Art. 26 CIT and the Pay and Refund mechanism above PLN 2M.
key_provisions: Art. 26 CIT — enhanced due diligence for related parties

concepts.definition: Distribution of profits from a Polish company to its foreign shareholder. Statutory WHT rate 19%. Potentially reduced under bilateral DTT or exempt under EU Parent-Subsidiary Directive (Art. 22 ust. 4 CIT) where ≥10% shareholding held for ≥2 years.
key_provisions: Art. 22 ust. 4 CIT — EU P-S Directive 0% exemption

concepts.definition: Payments for the use of money, typically on intragroup loans. Statutory WHT rate 20%. Potentially reduced under bilateral DTT or exempt under EU Interest and Royalties Directive 2003/49/EC (Art. 21 ust. 3 CIT) where ≥25% shareholding held for ≥2 years.
key_provisions: Art. 21 ust. 1 pkt 1 CIT — 20% statutory rate
Art. 21 ust. 3 CIT — EU I&R Directive 0% exemption

concepts.definition: Payments for the use of intellectual property: patents, trademarks, software, know-how, copyrights. Statutory WHT rate 20%. Potentially reduced under bilateral DTT or exempt under EU I&R Directive. Scope of 'royalty' is treaty-specific — Art. 12 OECD MC vs. Art. 12(3) French DTC difference is significant.
key_provisions: Art. 21 ust. 1 pkt 1 CIT — 20% statutory rate

concepts.term_en: headcount — employees in country of residence
concepts.definition: Number of employees physically present and working in the entity's country of registration. One of the universal MF Objaśnienia §2.3 substance criteria. Zero employees is the strongest conduit indicator. Tiered assessment: 0 = CONDUIT risk, 1-5 = WEAK, 6-50 = ADEQUATE, 51+ = STRONG (context-dependent).
key_provisions: MF Objaśnienia §2.3 — personel jako kryterium substancji
concepts.interview_question: How many employees does this entity have in its country of registration (people who actually work there)?

concepts.term_pl: fizyczna obecność w kraju siedziby — siedziba
concepts.definition: Whether the entity has a genuine physical office in its country of residence beyond a mere registered address (e.g., a law firm's address used as the registered office). A PO box or c/o address with no actual operations is a strong conduit indicator per MF Objaśnienia §2.3.
key_provisions: MF Objaśnienia §2.3 — siedziba jako kryterium substancji
concepts.interview_question: What type of office presence does this entity have in its country of registration?
interview_answer_bands: None / registered address only (e.g., law firm address)

concepts.definition: Whether the entity's board or management takes decisions independently in the country of residence, or whether all key decisions are made by the parent. A holding that is 100% directed by its parent has no independent decision-making and fails condition (iii). Key criterion per MF Objaśnienia §2.3.
key_provisions: MF Objaśnienia §2.3 — samodzielność w podejmowaniu decyzji
concepts.interview_question: How does this entity make business decisions — independently or directed by its parent?
interview_answer_bands: All major decisions made locally by entity management
Some decisions made locally, some directed by parent
All decisions made by parent / no local management

concepts.definition: The entity incurs genuine operating costs in its country of residence (salaries, rent, utilities, local professional fees). An entity that has no costs beyond a nominal registration fee has no economic reality. Assessed as a € range per MF Objaśnienia §2.3.
key_provisions: MF Objaśnienia §2.3 — koszty własne jako kryterium substancji
concepts.interview_question: What annual operating costs does this entity incur in its country of registration (salaries, rent, utilities)?

concepts.definition: The entity conducts genuine commercial operations — not just holding shares or passively receiving income. For a holding company, this means active management of subsidiaries with real decision-making. For an IP company, it means active R&D or licensing management, not just legal title to IP. The overarching standard against which all other substance factors are measured.

The entity bears genuine economic risks related to its assets and activities — market risk, credit risk, currency risk. An entity that is insulated from all risk by group guarantees and back-to-back arrangements lacks economic substance. Assessed under MF Objaśnienia §2.3 and directly linked to DEMPE control of risk under BEPS 8-10.
key_provisions: OECD TP Guidelines Ch. VI — control of risk

concepts.definition: An explicit contractual obligation requiring the entity to forward the received income to another party — the clearest form of pass-through. Automatically disqualifies BO status under condition (ii). Typical form: intercompany loan with back-to-back funding, IP sublicence with identical terms to upstream licence.

Even without a formal contractual obligation, a factual arrangement that achieves the same economic result — e.g., the entity forwards income to its parent within days of receipt, retaining no economic benefit — disqualifies BO status under condition (ii). This is the harder-to-detect form and the main focus of Polish WHT audits.
concepts.review_note: MF Objaśnienia §2.2.1 describes specific patterns for factual pass-through — verify exact list

concepts.definition: A conduit indicator: the entity receives X from Poland and forwards X (or X minus a thin margin) upstream. No economic benefit is retained beyond a nominal fee, suggesting the entity has no real economic interest in the income.

concepts.term_en: no economic risk attributable to the income stream
concepts.term_pl: brak ryzyka ekonomicznego związanego z należnością
concepts.definition: A conduit indicator: the entity bears no economic risk in relation to the income stream — it is guaranteed to receive and pass through the funds regardless of outcomes. Typical in back-to-back financing: the finance company earns a fixed spread and bears no credit risk, which is guaranteed by the parent.

Anti-avoidance rule introduced by MLI Article 7. A treaty benefit is denied if it is reasonable to conclude that obtaining that benefit was one of the principal purposes of an arrangement. Applies on top of the domestic BO test — even if a treaty rate applies, PPT can override it. Applies to Poland's treaties where both countries have ratified MLI and not excluded each other from Article 7.

A treaty provision (MLI Article 10 or treaty-specific) that restricts treaty benefits to entities that qualify under one of several objective tests (publicly traded, ownership and base erosion test, active business test, etc.). More mechanical than PPT. Poland has generally preferred PPT over LoB in its MLI positions.
concepts.review_note: Confirm Poland's MLI position on LoB — did Poland opt in to LOB under Art. 10?

concepts.definition: A structure where an entity is interposed in a tax treaty-favourable jurisdiction specifically to access treaty benefits that would not otherwise be available to the actual beneficial owner. The primary target of both the domestic BO test and the MLI PPT. Not per se illegal but triggers scrutiny.
key_provisions: OECD Commentary Art. 1 MC — treaty shopping

concepts.definition: Bilateral treaty between Poland and a foreign country that allocates taxing rights and limits Polish WHT rates on dividends, interest, and royalties. Poland has 91 active bilateral DTTs. Where a treaty applies, it overrides the statutory domestic rates (unless PPT or GAAR applies).

The rate at which the Polish paying entity is required to withhold tax from a payment to a foreign entity. Can be: statutory rate (19% dividends / 20% interest or royalties), reduced treaty rate (per DTT), or 0% under EU Directive. The correct rate depends on: treaty existence, BO status, MLI PPT, and Directive conditions.

Official document issued by the foreign tax authority confirming that an entity is a tax resident of a given country. Required by the Polish paying entity to apply a reduced treaty rate. Must be current (valid for the payment year). For third-party vendors, combined with a BO declaration, provides good-faith protection under MF Objaśnienia §4.
key_provisions: Art. 26 ust. 1 CIT — certyfikat rezydencji as condition for reduced rate

concepts.definition: Written declaration by the foreign entity confirming that it is the beneficial owner of the income. Required from related parties under Art. 26 CIT as part of enhanced due diligence. For unrelated third parties, combined with residence certificate, gives the paying entity good-faith protection even if BO status is later disputed.

The heightened due diligence standard required for related-party payments under Art. 26 CIT. A BO declaration alone is NOT sufficient — the paying entity must also verify substance, obtain financial statements, and assess the functional reality. This is the standard the agent is designed to support.
key_provisions: MF Objaśnienia §4 — standard dla podmiotów powiązanych

concepts.definition: Where a Polish paying entity has obtained the required documents (residence certificate + BO declaration) and has no reason to believe the information is incorrect, it is protected even if the preference is later found inapplicable. This protection is stronger for unrelated third parties and weaker for related parties where a higher verification standard applies.
key_provisions: MF Objaśnienia §4 — ochrona płatnika przy należytej staranności

concepts.definition: Where annual payments to a single related-party entity exceed PLN 2,000,000, the Polish entity must: (1) withhold at the statutory rate (not the reduced treaty/directive rate), and (2) the foreign entity claims a refund from the Polish tax authority. The paying entity can instead obtain: an Opinion (Opinia) from the tax authority certifying BO status, or a WH-OSC declaration signed by the board. Introduced 2022, key compliance burden for large intercompany payments.
key_provisions: Art. 26 §2c CIT — mechanizm poboru i zwrotu
Art. 26b CIT — Opinia o stosowaniu preferencji

concepts.definition: EU Council Directive 2003/49/EC providing 0% WHT on interest and royalties between associated EU companies where: both are EU resident, both are subject to corporate tax, and the payer holds ≥25% of the recipient (or vice versa) for ≥2 years. Implemented in Poland via Art. 21 ust. 3 CIT. The 0% rate applies only if the recipient is the beneficial owner.

EU Council Directive 2011/96/EU (recast) providing 0% WHT on dividends between associated EU companies where: both are EU resident, covered legal form, subject to corporate tax, and the payer holds ≥10% of the recipient for ≥2 years. Implemented in Poland via Art. 22 ust. 4 CIT.

The 'D' in DEMPE. The entity that actually performs or controls R&D activities that create the intangible asset should economically own and be compensated for those activities. Under BEPS 8-10 / OECD TP Guidelines Ch. VI, legal ownership of IP is insufficient — the entity that performs Development functions should earn the returns.
key_provisions: OECD TP Guidelines Ch. VI para. 6.32–6.39

concepts.definition: The 'E' in DEMPE. The entity that actively manages the commercial exploitation of the intangible — negotiating licences, monitoring licensee compliance, deciding pricing — performs exploitation functions. An IP holding that merely passively holds IP and auto-collects royalties may not perform meaningful exploitation functions.
key_provisions: OECD TP Guidelines Ch. VI para. 6.56–6.59

concepts.definition: Transactions between related parties should be priced as if they were between independent parties at arm's length. The foundation of transfer pricing analysis. For WHT purposes, arm's length pricing of intercompany royalties and interest affects whether the amount subject to WHT reflects genuine commercial terms or artificial profit shifting.

concepts.term_pl: Klauzula GAAR w podatku u źródła
concepts.definition: Domestic general anti-avoidance rule specific to WHT under Art. 22c CIT. Allows the tax authority to deny preferential WHT rates where the arrangement lacks genuine economic justification. Operates in parallel with the BO test and the MLI PPT — all three can apply independently.

Risk tier for third-party vendor screening: stable EU/EEA treaty, MLI PPT = NO or VERIFY (conservatively), Directive potentially applies, payment below PLN 2M threshold, entity is large publicly-listed company. Standard due diligence (residence cert + BO declaration) sufficient.
key_provisions: MF Objaśnienia §4 — standard należytej staranności dla podmiotów niepowiązanych

concepts.definition: Risk tier requiring enhanced due diligence: VERIFY MLI cases above PLN 2M, known conduit-risk jurisdictions (Delaware, Cayman, BVI, certain EU letterbox jurisdictions), missing treaty, payment above PLN 2M to related party. Triggers full substance assessment and likely Pay and Refund.
concepts.review_note: Known conduit jurisdictions list needs legal review — EU jurisdictions in particular are context-dependent, not inherently high-risk

concepts.definition: Entity has: 50+ employees in residence country, owned office premises, full decision-making independence, significant operating costs (>€1M), and strong evidence of genuine business activity. No pass-through indicators. Maximum BO confidence. Typical profile: large publicly-listed operating company (e.g. Orange S.A.).

Entity has: zero or near-zero employees, no genuine physical office, no independent decision-making, minimal operating costs, with one or more pass-through indicators present. HIGH likelihood of BO test failure. Typical profile: a Luxembourg or BVI holding with a single nominee director.
